description:
Available for a transatlantic Charter to go back to the Med
Summer 2026: South of France, Italy, Sicily, Sardinia
Available for a transatlantic Charter to go to the Caribbean
Winter 2026/2027 : Caribbean
Spring : TBC - Costa Rica / Panama
Summer 2027 : TBC - French Polynesia 

ATLANTIC is a 64.50m / 212ft Sailing Superyacht built in 2010 by Van Der Graaf BV. Sleeping 12 guests in 6 cabins and with a crew of 12. Cruising at 10.00 kn/ph with a top speed of 12.00 kn/ph. The guest cabins comprise of a Master cabin, 2 double cabins, 1 twin cabin, and 2 bunk-bed cabins all with en-suite bathroom facilities.

blueprint.name: ATLANTIC

toys.label: Bicycles (for both onshore exploration and onboard use with a home trainer)

tenders: 6.5 m SILLINGER + 115 HP MERCURY outboard engine

crew.name: Fosse Fortuin
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Fosse boasts impressive and extensive experience in the luxury yachting industry, having captained and crewed on numerous prestigious vessels throughout his career. His career highlights include serving as captain of S/Y WYLDE SWAN, S/Y GLORIA, S/V BARK EUROPA and S/Y HELIOS, as well as taking on relief captain roles for yachts such as S/Y ELENA OF LONDON and S/Y MONDANGO. In addition to his leadership at sea, Fosse has held the role of Yard Captain for Vitters Shipyard, where he oversaw sea trials and commissioning for new builds like S/Y MERAKI and S/Y MAXIMUS. Currently, he applies his wealth of expertise as captain of the legendary S/Y ATLANTIC, where he has successfully led six charter seasons in the Mediterranean and UK since 2018.

Job Description 
Fosse is responsible for navigation, safety, the overall running and financial management of the vessel, crew and guests. He ensures the safe and efficient operation of the yacht at all times, including compliance with all safety procedures and regulations. He takes care of adhering to flag regulations, port state and local laws and regulations, and manages the crew to ensure adherence to, and a full understanding of, the vessel’s policies. He provides daily leadership for the crew and encourages team-work, individual growth and personal attention by delegating duties and supervising all crew tasks.

Qualifications and Certificates :
•  Certificate of Competency: Master Mariner Unlimited, including Polar Code Certification
•  Trading Area: Unlimited (Merchant)
•  GMDSS General Radio Operator
•  STCW Codes: II/2, IV/2, A-VI-3, A-II/2, A-VI/4 (Commercially Endorsed)
•  Ship Management (STCW)
•  Advanced Fire Fighting (STCW)
•  Radar Management (STCW)
•  ECDIS (STCW)
•  Polar Code Certification (STCW)

crew.name: Alex Drummond
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Alex is a seasoned Captain with over 35 years’ experience and more than 360,000 nautical miles sailed. He has commanded both motor and sailing yachts up to 93m, with extensive experience in new builds, major refits, and classic yacht restoration. He spent 15 years as Captain of S/Y BARE NECESSITIES, overseeing her build, commercial certification, and a highly successful charter programme, remaining on board through a change of ownership. More recently, he has managed complex refits and deliveries, including a 43m classic S/Y and a 45m Ice Class M/Y, as well as rotational and relief roles on both private and commercial vessels. His cruising experience spans the Mediterranean, Caribbean, North Atlantic, and Northern Europe, and he brings strong leadership, technical expertise, and a proven ability to run safe, efficient, and professional yacht operations. 

Job Description
Alex is responsible for navigation, safety, the overall running and financial management of the vessel, crew and guests. He ensures the safe and efficient operation of the yacht at all times, including compliance with all safety procedures and regulations. He takes care of adhering to flag regulations, port state and local laws and regulations, and manages the crew to ensure adherence to, and a full understanding of, the vessel’s policies. He provides daily leadership for the crew and encourages team-work, individual growth and personal attention by delegating duties and supervising all crew tasks.

Licences and Qualifications
•	MCA Master 3000GT (2002, renewed 2023)
•	STCW (all updates completed 2022)
•	MCA Boatman’s Certificate
•	ECDIS (Generic, Transas & Furuno – updated 2025)
•	Pilot Exemption Certificates – Southampton & Cowes
•	RYA Yachtmaster Offshore & Ocean
•	Diploma in Boat Building (Distinction), Falmouth Boat Building College
•	Yacht Brokerage Certification
•	Mental Health Awareness Course for Captains

crew.name: Michael Pannone
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Michael is a seasoned sailor with extensive experience in both sailing and motor yachts. He has most recently worked aboard the 65m classic S/Y ATLANTIC as First Mate during the Mediterranean charter season and classic regattas. Prior to that, he served as First Mate on the 35m M/Y KAYCEE, where he was also responsible for engineering duties. He captained his own 12m yacht, SV CALICOBA, on a multi-year voyage from France to Australia, managing over 40 crew along the way. His background also includes chase boat captain and fishing guide roles on M/Y MERCIELAGO. Michael brings a robust skill set in sailing, mechanics, electrics, shipwright work, and crew leadership.

Job Description
Michael supports the captain in the operation and navigation of the yacht, overseeing deck operations, safety procedures, and maintenance tasks. He coordinates sailing maneuvers, leads the deck crew, and ensures all systems are operating efficiently. With strong communication skills and practical knowledge, he plays a vital role in the smooth and safe functioning of the vessel.

Licences and Qualifications
• AMSA Master 5
• IYT 200GT
• AMSA MED 2
• STCW
• Powerboat Level 2
• PADI Dive Instructor 

Languages:
English

crew.name: Amarja Koers
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Amarja is an accomplished Chief Stewardess and Purser with extensive experience on both private and charter yachts ranging from 45M to 67M. She has led the interior teams on prestigious vessels including M/Y MARIA (67M AMELS), S/Y ATHOS (64M schooner), and M/Y LARS (47M explorer yacht). Her roles have encompassed running busy dual-season programs, new owner setups post-refit, high-profile guest trips, and regatta participation such as the St. Barth’s Bucket. She has strong event planning capabilities, proven during multiple high-end guest trips featuring live entertainment and private instructors. Amarja’s earlier yachting career also includes charter experience on S/Y RHEA and S/Y CHRONOS, where she structured interior service protocols and trained junior stews. Prior to yachting, she worked as a biology teacher and managed high-end hospitality operations, adding strong leadership and service acumen to her profile.

Job Description
Amarja is responsible for the full management of the interior department, including housekeeping, laundry, guest service, and event execution. She creates and maintains inventories, budgets, work schedules, and planned maintenance programs. In her purser capacity, she handles financial administration, guest itinerary preparation, crew logistics, and hiring processes. Amarja provides leadership and mentorship for her team, ensuring the highest standards of guest satisfaction and seamless onboard operations.

Licenses and Qualifications
• IAMI GUEST Yacht Purser Program
• WSET Level 2 Award in Wines
• STCW10 Basic Safety Training + Refresher 
• ENG1 Seafarer Medical Certificate 
• STCW Proficiency in Designated Security Duties
• Food Safety and Hygiene HABC Level 2
• PADI Open Water Diver Certification

Languages
English, Dutch

crew.name: Sam Mackenzie
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Sam is a seasoned private chef with over a decade of experience crafting high-end cuisine in luxury private chalets in the French Alps. For the past two summers, he has transitioned his talents to the yachting world, working aboard a 50-meter superyacht. His culinary background reflects a deep understanding of seasonal and global cuisine, and he is committed to delivering exceptional, personalized dining experiences both on land and at sea.

Job Description
Sam is responsible for provisioning, menu planning, and preparing meals for guests and crew. He focuses on delivering high-quality cuisine tailored to guest preferences, dietary needs, and seasonal availability. He ensures the galley is efficiently run, maintains hygiene standards, and collaborates closely with other crew members to align culinary service with the yacht's hospitality experience.

Licenses and Qualifications
    • STCW Basic Safety Training and Medical
    • ENG1
    • Level 2 Food Hygiene and Safety for Catering

Languages
English, French, Maori

crew.name: Antonio Argueta
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Chef Antonio is passionate about food, wine, and travel. He states, "food, wine and travel make my life perfect". His global travels have given him valuable insights into various cuisines and wines, which he incorporates into his recipes. Since joining ATLANTIC in 2019, Antonio has provided exceptional service to our guests.

Job Description
Antonio is responsible for provisioning, menu planning, and preparing meals for guests and crew. He focuses on delivering high-quality cuisine tailored to guest preferences, dietary needs, and seasonal availability. He ensures the galley is efficiently run, maintains hygiene standards, and collaborates closely with other crew members to align culinary service with the yacht's hospitality experience.

Languages: Spanish, Italian, French, English

crew.name: Pim van Dijk
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Pim is a skilled engineer with a deep technical background and several years of experience in the maritime sector, particularly aboard traditional sailing vessels. Since 2019, he has served as engineer on tall ships including WYLDE SWAN, NOORDERLICHT, and ARTEMIS. Pim brings a wealth of hands-on knowledge in maintaining and repairing hydraulic, mechanical, electrical, and pneumatic systems, honed through his earlier career in the heavy crane industry with Van Schie B.V. and Mammoet B.V., where he handled complex repairs and international projects.

Job Description
Pim is responsible for the maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of all onboard technical systems. He ensures that all mechanical, electrical, hydraulic, and safety systems operate smoothly and efficiently. His calm, observant nature and problem-solving mindset make him highly effective in keeping the vessel in optimal condition during voyages and charters.

Licences and Qualifications
• Yachtmaster Coastal
• Klein Vaarbewijs II
• Stuurman Kleine Zeilvaart (Small Sailing Vessel Officer) – Enkhuizer Zeevaartschool
• Medical Care & Medical First Aid
• STCW Basic Safety Training
• Freediving Level 1 (SSI)

crew.name: Josselin Gaudin
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Josselin is a highly experienced sailor with a strong background in classic and luxury sailing yachts. He has served as Bosun aboard ATLANTIC since 2017, where he has been deeply involved in sailing, charter operations, maneuvering, and ongoing deck and rigging maintenance. His earlier roles include Galley/Deckhand on S/Y GERMANIA NOVA, and OOW on two transatlantic deliveries aboard S/Y STATE OF GRACE, where he also handled deck maintenance and boat show preparation. Josselin has also worked briefly as an engineer on M/Y LADY MONA K and held several deckhand roles aboard various yachts and a ferry during his formative years. His experience spans hands-on rigging, varnish and paint work, safety operations, and guest services.

Job Description
Josselin supports the captain in all navigational and operational aspects of the yacht. He oversees deck operations, sailing manoeuvres, maintenance of rigging and deck equipment, and assists with safety drills and protocols. As First Mate, he plays a key leadership role among deck crew, ensuring smooth and safe operations during charters and passages, while maintaining the vessel to the highest standard.

Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW95 Basic Safety
• Advanced Firefighting
• G.M.D.S.S
• B.A.E.E.R.S
• Radar A.P.R
• Powerboat License
• ENG 1
• PADI Open Water

Languages
French, English

crew.name: Claire Milovan
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Claire is a dedicated crew member with experience across both deck and interior roles on a range of sailing yachts. Claire has completed transatlantic crossings, deliveries, and private cruises aboard yachts such as S/Y BENETEAU OCEANIS, S/Y TIBITOO, and S/Y GOLOK, where she assisted with deck procedures, watch-keeping, provisioning, and meal preparation. Her versatility, seamanship, and eagerness to contribute make her a valuable asset on board. In her free time she enjoys cooking, horse-riding, hiking and climbing.

Job Description
Claire assists with the daily operation and maintenance of the yacht, performing duties such as deck procedures, watchkeeping, and general upkeep. She also supports the interior team with service, housekeeping, and cooking. Her adaptability allows her to move confidently between interior and exterior responsibilities, contributing to smooth onboard operations and an enjoyable guest experience.
Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW 
• ENG1 
• Yacht Interior Service (G.U.E.S.T), Lycée Paul Augier
• Advanced Bartending and Mixology
• Yacht Galley Operation

Languages
French, English, Spanish

crew.name: Jord Bakker
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Jord is an enthusiastic and reliable deckhand with hands-on experience aboard sailing yachts. He served as a Temporary Deck Hand on SY MERAKI from September to November 2024, where he took responsibility for servicing and cleaning sailing equipment and deck hardware, as well as managing inventories of deck spare parts. He also participated in a crossing aboard SY MERAKI in November 2024, where he was involved in watchkeeping, engine room checks, logbook maintenance, and radar monitoring. In his free time, Jord enjoys playing golf at his local club for relaxation and staying active through paddle.

Job Description
Jord assists in the general deck operations of the vessel, including maintenance, sailing maneuvers, rigging, and tendering. He supports guest activities, helps with docking procedures, and contributes to interior management when required. 

Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW 
• ENG1 Medical Certificate 
• Powerboat Level 2

Languages
English, Dutch

crew.name: Quinten Stienstra
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Quinten is a young but experienced sailor. He has completed a transatlantic crossing as a trainee on the schooner NOORDERLICHT and worked for five months as a deckhand aboard the WYLDE SWAN, including one month focused on vessel maintenance. He has additional sailing experience in the North Sea, Baltic Sea, English Channel, Sea of the Hebrides, and Atlantic Ocean. Quinten also has competitive dinghy sailing experience at national and regional levels and has worked as a dinghy sailing instructor. He has performed daywork on motor yachts in St. Maarten and has experience in anchoring, rigging, tender operations, engineering support, and line splicing.

Job Description
Quinten assists in the general deck operations of the vessel, including maintenance, sailing maneuvers, rigging, and tendering. He supports guest activities, helps with docking procedures, and contributes to interior management when required. His enthusiasm and broad base of practical skills make him a valuable addition to any crew.

Licences and Qualifications
• CoC STCW II/4
• STCW-95
• Medical Certificate 
• Seaman’s Book 
• CWO V Keelboat and Dinghy

Languages
Dutch, English, German

crew.name: Fanny Meleder
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Fanny is an experienced and enthusiastic stewardess with a background in both motor and sailing yachts. She started her work aboard ATLANTIC from March, where she has been involved in guest service, interior and exterior maintenance, and deck work including varnish sanding. Her other yachting experience includes working on M/Y BREEZE (Ferretti 720) across two charter seasons, handling client service, interior detailing, and supporting mooring operations. She also gained experience aboard M/Y WEST FIELD (Riva Virtus 63), and has completed a hands-on training course aboard M/Y SEXY FISH. In addition to yachting, Fanny has worked in hospitality and retail, and brings strong interpersonal and service skills to her roles.

Job Description
Fanny is responsible for maintaining a high standard of cleanliness and guest service on board, managing both interior detailing and assisting with deck duties when needed. She handles guest welcoming, service, inventory, and supports the team during mooring and other operations. Her combined hospitality background and maritime knowledge make her a versatile and dependable crew member.

Licences and Qualifications
• Professional Quality Certificate Stewardess
• STCW 10
• ENG1
• Deck Rating 
• Medical I 

Languages
French, English, Spanish

crew.name: Libby Rackham
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Libby has built a solid foundation in the yachting industry, initially joining M/Y BACHATA QUEEN, a 35m Codecasa classic yacht, as 2nd Stewardess. There, she worked as part of a small crew, preparing the yacht for the season by detailing guest cabins, managing laundry, provisioning, and assisting on deck. She later undertook interior daywork aboard M/Y FABULOUS CHARACTER, a 43m Holland Jachtbouw build, focusing on turning around the main areas and crew accommodations after an Atlantic crossing. Libby’s background in high-end hospitality, including luxury chalet hosting in Courchevel, has also strengthened her capabilities in service, housekeeping, and guest relations.

Job Description
Libby is responsible for the maintenance of high standards within the interior, including guest service, housekeeping, laundry, and provisioning. She supports the Chief Stewardess or Captain in ensuring the guest experience is seamless, with a strong focus on attention to detail, discretion, and proactive service. She also assists occasionally on deck operations when required.

Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW 
• ENG1 
• Proficiency in Security Awareness
• Proficiency in Designated Security Duties
• RYA Powerboat Level 2
• Mental Health First Aid
• RYA VHF Radio
• CIEH Food Hygiene Level 2
• WSET Level 2 in Wine and Spirits (Merit)
• Flying Fish Superyacht Stewardess Course

Languages
English
